title: Walsh vetoes more than half of Syracuse lawmakers' budget amendments
summary: Mayor Ben Walsh vetoed 45 of 82 budget amendments (55%) that Syracuse Common Council passed, reducing a proposed $348M budget by $16M. Walsh prioritized protecting public safety funding and revenue-generation initiatives while blocking elimination of a 2% property tax increase and water rate increases. Council has two weeks to override vetoes, needing only six votes, with Walsh signaling willingness to negotiate.

political_signal.description: Mayor exercised veto authority to protect public safety and revenue operations against council cost-cutting; explicitly signaled willingness to compromise and negotiate. Council passed cuts unanimously but lacks override supermajority certainty.

processing_notes: Article date conflict: headline says May 20, 2025 news conference but publish date is May 19, 2025—likely conference scheduled after publication
Dollar amounts need unit clarification: $348M (budget), $16M (cuts), $5 (water rate increase per unit/month inferred)
Key deadline: 14 days for council final action
Vote math: unanimous passage of amendments (all 9 council members) but only 6 needed to override veto suggests political leverage shift
